Abel Mendoza
Failure Analysis Technician | Electronics Test and Validation | Hardware Troubleshooting
Anaheim, CA
[test.candidate@example.com](mailto:test.candidate@example.com) | (562) 322-2299
linkedin.com/in/test-candidate
Professional Summary
Electronics and robotics technician with hands-on experience performing hardware validation, troubleshooting, failure
isolation, root cause analysis, component-level rework, and corrective action across industrial electronics and autonomous
robotic systems. Experienced with DC-DC converter testing, relay logic validation, embedded hardware bring-up, robotics
deployment support, and diagnostic testing using programmable electronic loads, bench power supplies, multimeters,
and network diagnostic tools. Strong background working with electromechanical systems, documenting findings, and
collaborating with engineering teams to improve reliability, quality, and product performance.
Technical Skills
Failure Analysis and Troubleshooting: Root Cause Analysis, Failure Isolation, Hardware Validation, Board-Level
Debugging, Reliability Analysis, Diagnostic Testing, Corrective Actions
Electronics and Embedded Systems: DC-DC Converters, Relay Logic Circuits, PIC32 Microcontrollers, Raspberry
Pi, FPGA Tooling, Embedded Linux, UART, SPI, I2C, GPIO
Electronics Rework and Assembly: Component-Level PCB Rework, Through-Hole Soldering, Resistor and Capacitor
Replacement, Inspection, Repair Verification
Test Equipment: Digital Multimeters, Bench Power Supplies, Programmable Electronic Loads, Optical Power Meters,
Continuity Testing
Programming: Python, C++, Bash, JavaScript
Documentation: Technical Reports, Validation Records, Test Procedures, Engineering Documentation
Professional Experience
Electronics Test and Validation Technician Apr 2026 – May 2026
RLH Industries, Orange, CA
• Performed root cause investigation, troubleshooting, and validation testing on industrial electronic assemblies including
DC-DC power converters, relay systems, and fiber optic communication hardware
• Performed component-level PCB rework, replacing and re-soldering resistors, capacitors, and other electronic compo-
nents that failed quality inspection or electrical testing, improving product reliability and test yield
• Conducted board-level failure isolation using multimeters, programmable electronic loads, continuity testing, embedded
diagnostics, and known-good reference hardware
• Executed DC-DC converter validation including voltage regulation testing, load characterization, tolerance verification,
and thermal drift analysis
• Validated optical communication systems operating at 850nm and 1310nm wavelengths using calibrated optical power
meters
• Diagnosed hardware anomalies, documented findings, and communicated results to engineering teams to support cor-
rective actions and product reliability improvements
• Performed firmware flashing, embedded hardware bring-up, and industrial Ethernet device deployment using PIC32-
based systems, MPLAB ICD3, MPFS2, USB Blaster, and Quartus
Robotics Deployment Technician Jan 2025 – Dec 2025
Armstrong Robotics, Hollywood, CA
• Diagnosed and resolved hardware, sensor, communication, and control system failures during live deployment of au-
tonomous robotic systems
• Performed failure investigation and corrective actions across mechanical, electrical, and electromechanical subsystems
operating in production environments
• Replaced, calibrated, and validated robotic grippers and automation hardware to maintain operational reliability
• Collaborated directly with engineering teams to identify recurring failure modes and improve system performance

-- 1 of 2 --

• Documented operational issues and troubleshooting procedures to support maintenance and future deployments
Field Robotics Agent Mar 2025 – May 2025
Serve Robotics, Los Angeles, CA
• Investigated hardware, sensor, navigation, and connectivity failures affecting autonomous delivery robots in live field
operations
• Performed systematic troubleshooting and verification testing to restore operational status
• Escalated technical findings to engineering teams and contributed data supporting reliability improvements
Technical Projects
Omega-1 Autonomous Robotics Platform
Raspberry Pi 4B | Python | Embedded Linux
• Integrated motor controllers, sensors, power systems, and embedded hardware into a custom autonomous robotics
platform
• Diagnosed communication, timing, and signal integrity issues affecting servo control, sensor performance, and system
stability
• Performed hardware validation and debugging across power distribution, motor control, ultrasonic sensing, and vision
subsystems
• Developed diagnostic tools to monitor system health and validate hardware performance
